stories.headline: SpaceX is buying Cursor for $60 billion
stories.blurb: Days after its IPO, SpaceX announced it's acquiring the AI coding tool Cursor for $60 billion. The deal is Elon Musk's bet to win enterprise customers and compete directly with Anthropic and OpenAI in the AI market.
stories.why_it_matters: The biggest AI coding tool just became part of Musk's empire.
stories.original_headline: SpaceX is officially buying Cursor for $60 billion

stories.headline: Indian AI startup Sarvam hits $1B valuation with $234M from HCLTech
stories.blurb: Sarvam, a Bengaluru-based AI company, raised $234 million led by Indian IT giant HCLTech's $150 million investment. The round makes Sarvam India's newest AI unicorn, joining the handful of billion-dollar-valued AI companies emerging outside Silicon Valley.
stories.why_it_matters: India now has its own billion-dollar AI bet, signaling the global AI race extends beyond US and Chinese labs.
stories.original_headline: Sarvam becomes India’s newest AI unicorn with $234 million funding round led by HCLTech

stories.headline: Hackers stole 2FA codes from Copilot users through search hijacking
stories.blurb: A vulnerability called SearchLeak let attackers inject invisible text into Copilot results that tricked users into leaking their two-factor codes. The exploit worked by poisoning search results with hidden instructions that made Copilot ask users to share sensitive data — then send it to attacker-controlled servers.
stories.why_it_matters: LLMs can be weaponized through their own search features, turning helpful AI into a phishing vector.
stories.original_headline: Critical Copilot vulnerability allowed hackers to seal 2FA code from users

stories.headline: DeepSeek raises $7.4 billion, becomes China's most valuable AI startup
stories.blurb: China's DeepSeek closed a $7.4 billion funding round—its first external raise—to bankroll AI development. The company, which released competitive open models last year while claiming minimal compute costs, is now the country's highest-valued AI startup.
stories.why_it_matters: DeepSeek went from efficiency darling to needing Silicon Valley-scale capital overnight.
stories.original_headline: DeepSeek Becomes China’s Most Valuable AI Startup After $7.4 Billion Fundraise

stories.headline: Arcade raises $60M to build guardrails for AI agents
stories.blurb: The startup helps companies control what their AI agents can actually do — like which APIs they can call, what data they can access, and which actions require human approval. As more businesses deploy agents that can take real actions, deciding what to let them do becomes a security problem.
stories.why_it_matters: Agent permissions are the new access control layer companies need to figure out now
stories.original_headline: Arcade.dev Raises $60 Million to Secure AI Agents

stories.headline: Qualcomm CEO: AI agents will replace apps on 40 new devices
stories.blurb: Qualcomm's CEO says the company is building 40 AI-powered devices where agents handle tasks instead of apps. He's especially bullish on smart glasses, which he thinks could become as big as smartphones.
stories.why_it_matters: The shift from apps to agents is moving from software into the hardware you'll actually buy.
stories.original_headline: Qualcomm CEO says AI agents will replace apps — as chip giant works on 40 new AI-powered devices

try_this_prompt.title: The negotiation floor-finder
try_this_prompt.prompt: I'm negotiating something I'll describe below. I need you to tell me: the absolute minimum I can accept without feeling cheated in six months, the one thing I should ask for that they'll probably say yes to, and the exact sentence I should use if they go silent.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: Before you say a number out loud or hit send on that counteroffer.

daily_question: If AI agents replace apps, what's the first app on your phone you'd actually trust an agent to handle?
